Design and Characterization of a Gunshot Detection Circuit Using BJT Preamplifier

E. [Last], K. Kamali, A. [Last]

Department of Electrical and Computer Engineering, University of Maryland

Abstract: This paper presents the design and characterization of a gunshot detection circuit based on an electret condenser microphone (CMA-4544PF-W) and a common-emitter BJT preamplifier (2N2222A). Results demonstrate reliable detection at 5-10m with peak output voltages of 1.2-1.9V. Frequency domain analysis reveals energy between 2-20 kHz.

ENEB304 Microelectronics & Sensors | Dr. Nestor Michael C. Tiglao | Spring 2026

I. Introduction

Acoustic gunshot detection is critical for law enforcement, military surveillance, and urban safety monitoring.

Commercial systems cost $50K-$100K per square mile. We investigate a minimal circuit approach.

Contributions

1. CE amplifier design matched to electret microphone impedance

2. Experimental characterization under three conditions

3. Time and frequency domain analysis of captured signals

II. Circuit Design

Voltage Gain:      Av = -gm * RC = -(IC/VT) * RC = -385
Input Impedance:   Rin = rpi = (beta * VT) / IC = 2.6 kOhm
Bandwidth:         723 Hz to 777 kHz
Impedance Match:   Zmic(2.2k) to Zin(2.6k) = 99.3% efficiency

IV. Results

ParameterFarMediumCap-Coupled
Peak (mV)128018801200
RMS (mV)193165259
Peak/RMS6.611.44.6
Dom. Freq (kHz)2.36.920.0

V. Conclusions

A single-stage CE BJT amplifier using 2N2222A reliably detects gunshots at 5-10m.

Measured gain is consistent with theoretical predictions (Av ~ -385).

Frequency content spans 2-20 kHz, within amplifier bandwidth.

References

[1] Sedra & Smith, Microelectronic Circuits, 8th ed. | [2] Horowitz & Hill, Art of Electronics, 3rd ed.

[3] ON Semi, PN2222A Datasheet | [4] CUI, CMA-4544PF-W Datasheet | [5] Lyons, Understanding DSP, 3rd ed.
